diagrams.title: EOF Detection Methods Comparison
diagrams.description: Shows four different methods to detect EOF in C++ with their pros and cons
diagrams.purpose: Help students choose the right EOF detection method for their use case

diagrams.title: Stream State Flags Flow
diagrams.description: Shows progression of stream states from good to bad
elements: goodbit: No errors, stream is good
failbit: Logical failure (includes EOF + type mismatch)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how stream state changes during file operations

diagrams.title: Reading Past EOF Timeline
diagrams.description: Timeline showing what happens when attempting to read past end of file
elements: [4] Attempt read past EOF - eofbit+failbit set
diagrams.purpose: Understand when EOF flags are actually set (not at end of data but after failed read attempt)

diagrams.title: Binary vs Text File EOF Detection
diagrams.description: Comparison of EOF handling in text and binary files
elements: Text file: May use EOF marker characters, uses eof()/fail() functions
Binary file: No EOF marker, uses stream state or gcount()
Key difference: 0-255 valid data in binary mode vs special characters in text
diagrams.purpose: Highlight challenges in detecting EOF for different file types
